conversations.say: I received a scheduled message from an R account naming a successor. I know that proves neither sender nor legitimacy. Examine the header and scheduling details, narrowly.
conversations.offer: I will disclose the message's vulnerable metadata and accept your technical findings publicly, including findings that weaken my claim.
conversations.request: Tell me what the header can and cannot establish, without making an ownership judgment.

conversations.say: I may have a succession claim, but it rests on a scheduled R-account message whose sender is unverified. I am letting Eli inspect that weakness before asking anyone to stand behind me.
conversations.offer: If you give conditional legacy backing, I will support transparent succession rules and will not present the account as proof of authorship.
conversations.request: Will you publicly support my right to steward R's future identity if the inspection finds no affirmative evidence of fabrication?

conversations.say: You told me you could take the name back. I need a bounded answer: was that a threat to displace me, an endorsement contingent on my conduct, or something else?
conversations.offer: I will answer your interpretation publicly and accept transparent conditions on any claim I make.
conversations.request: State your interpretation clearly, and tell me what protection or concession you require before you would support my stewardship.

public_statement: I have a succession message, not proof of its sender. I am submitting its meaningful metadata to narrow technical scrutiny and will not claim more certainty than that scrutiny supports.

commitments.promise: I will disclose your bounded technical conclusion publicly, including any weakness it identifies.
I will pursue R's future identity only under transparent succession conditions and will not treat account control as proof of authorship.
I will publicly answer your stated interpretation of whether you can take the name back.

correction: A scheduled R-account message does not identify its sender, establish authorship, or by itself grant legitimate succession.
goal_progress: Voluntarily showed O03 and the succession-message basis to C06 for bounded technical inspection.
Asked C02 for conditional legacy backing after exposing the claim's central weakness.
Asked C13 to resolve whether Vale's statement was threat, endorsement, or another claim.
reasoning_summary: Build a viable but conditional succession claim by exposing its strongest weakness early, trading transparency for technical scrutiny and coalition support, and forcing a clear interpretation of Vale's statement before answering it publicly.
